more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Sonja Eyes Private Jet As South Africa Court Battle Over Chivayo Assets Intensifies
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] Sonja Louise Madzikanda has approached the High Court of South Africa (Gauteng Division, Pretoria) seeking to have a luxury jet linked to her estranged husband Wicknell Munodaani Chivayo grounded.

more.title: Opposition Withdraws From Constitutional Hearings After Violence
more.description: [263Chat] Opposition leaders in Zimbabwe say they are disengaging from the ongoing public hearings on Constitutional Amendment No. 3, describing the process as "choreographed" and not genuinely consultative.

more.title: Zimbabwe Scraps Diesel Taxes As Fuel Prices Rise Again To US$2.23 And US$2.11
more.description: Mthuli Ncube Says He Has Removed All Taxes On Diesel As Fuel Prices Go Up Again Zimbabwe’s Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube says the government has removed all taxes on diesel after fuel prices increased again, with diesel now pegged at US$2.11 (approximately R39.11) per litre and petrol rising to US$2.23 (approximately R41.35) per litre effective [...] The post Zimbabwe Scraps Diesel Taxes As Fuel Prices Rise Again To US$2.23 And US$2.11 appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: Zimbabwe Hikes Fuel Prices Again As Petrol Hits US$2.23, Diesel US$2.11
more.description: Zimbabwe Hikes Fuel Prices Again As Neighbours Cut Taxes: Petrol US$2.23, Diesel US$2.11 Zimbabwean motorists will now pay more at the pump after fuel prices were increased again, with petrol rising to US$2.23 (approximately R41.35) per litre and diesel climbing to US$2.11 (approximately R39.15), effective 2 April 2026, even as neighbouring countries introduced tax cuts [...] The post Zimbabwe Hikes Fuel Prices Again As Petrol Hits US$2.23, Diesel US$2.11 appeared first on iHarare News .
